issues.body: review_outcome calls record_round_void for every result other than `ok`, but revalidate_outcome_head also returns `closed`, `author`, `undrafted`, `malformed-head`, and `unbound`. Those are not legitimate head churn. In particular, a missing/malformed `--reviewed-sha` after the model ran now voids the pre-model charge, so the sweep can repeatedly rerun the same deterministic wiring/identity failure without consuming `max_review_rounds`; wrong-author or undraft tampering likewise weakens accounting instead of preserving the human/tamper stop. The updated tests explicitly bless this for author and undrafted states, but do not assert that identity failures remain charged. This contradicts the stated bounded-crash/fail-closed invariant.
issues.fix_hint: Only write a round-void marker when `freshness == "head-moved"` (and ideally verify the exact bot-authored `(round, run)` marker is bound to `args.reviewed_sha`). For closed, author, undrafted, malformed-head, and unbound outcomes, defer or fail closed without voiding the charge. Update the outcome self-tests to assert a void for head-moved only and no void for every other freshness reason, especially malformed/missing reviewed SHA.
issues.title: All freshness failures erase the charged round instead of only a moved head

verdict.summary: The prior round's SHA-binding defect is fixed: --head-sha is required, record_round rejects malformed values before posting, and the self-test exercises both rejection and a valid bound marker. However, the new void path is broader than head churn. Every freshness failure—including malformed or missing reviewed SHA, wrong author, closed/undrafted state, and malformed live head—mints a void and removes the attempt from the crash budget. That converts identity/wiring failures and tamper stops into uncharged retries, contrary to fail-closed bounded accounting. Because this is a new major issue while the prior major was repaired, progress is regressing under the required scale.
